page: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-intel is an entry-level virtual machine within the High Performance server family. It features an Intel Xeon Cascadelake x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, supported by 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 60 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU is included.

Benchmark results indicate modest processing capabilities. The server achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1384.38 and a multi-core score of 2740.38, both placing in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory performance is constrained, with large-block read bandwidth at 13.19 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 11.09 GB/sec, placing in the bottom 10%. Database operations via Passmark scored 606.27, and Redis SET operations reached 2.81 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is recorded at 2.37 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources or high memory density. The shared CPU allocation and 2.0 GB RAM limit its suitability for resource-intensive applications, presenting a clear tradeoff between low operational cost and restricted performance.

This server is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, small-scale development or testing environments, simple automation scripts, and lightweight background tasks that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling and low memory availability.
description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-intel is a budget-oriented virtual server featuring an Intel Xeon Cascadelake x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 2.0 GB of RAM and 60 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data positions this instance in the lower performance tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2740.38 and a Passmark database operations score of 606.27. Large-block memory read bandwidth is limited to 13.19 GB/sec. Given its shared CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio, this server represents a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and low-intensity background tasks.
